content:
Issue No. 21/2017 March EFY2017
--------------------------------------------------
1. Summary
The Consumer Price Index (CPI), a key economic statistics, is surveyed, analyzed, and
made available to data users on a monthly basis.
The year-on-year, general inflation rate (annual percentage change) in the country stood at
13.6 percent for the month of March EFY2017. A year earlier (in March EFY2016), the rate
was 26.2 percent. The month-on-month general inflation rate increased by 2.7 percent for
the month of March-EFY2017. It was 4.0 percent in March EFY2016.
General inflation encompasses of two main divisions and twenty-one groups of expendi-
tures. These divisions registered the following price changes during the month of March
EFY2017 compared with March EFY2016.
2. Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages and Non-Food Inflation (Year-on-Year)
The year-on-year inflation and the month-to-month inflation rates helps to gauge the cur-
rent inflation trajectory, as they capture short-term fluctuations in prices and provides an
immediate indicator of inflationary pressures. Particularly, the month-to-month is also cru-
cial for monitoring changes in consumer prices and identifying any sudden spikes or de-
clines in inflation.
The year-on-year inflation and the month-to-month inflation rates helps to gauge the cur-
rent inflation trajectory, as they capture short-term fluctuations in prices and provides an
immediate indicator of inflationary pressures. Particularly, the month-to-month is also cru-
cial for monitoring changes in consumer prices and identifying any sudden spikes or de-
clines in inflation.
Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages index
The year-on-year inflation rate, also known as the annual inflation rate, measures the per-
centage change in overall price levels from one year to the same month in the previous year.
Accordingly, the year-on-year inflation rate of the Food and Non-alcoholic component of the
CPI, for the month of March 2017 EFY stood at 11.9%. This implies that the CPI of Food and
Non-Alcoholic Beverages has, on average, increased by 11.9% compared to March EFY2016.
The major contributing factors for the rise of food inflation for March EFY2017 was the ob-
served increase in the average prices of major food commodities such as bread and cereals
(1.2%), vegetables (6.1%), meat (17.8%), Sugar, jam, honey & chocolate (28.8%), Milk, Cheese
and Eggs (8.2%), Oils and Fats (36.3%), Fruit (13.4%), and Food products n.e.c (26.7%).

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 2 |  |  |  | 
--------------------------------------------------
Beginning from March-EFY 2016 the magnitude of the inflation rate steadily declined up to March-EFY
2017. In March EFY-2017 General inflation was 13.6% as compared to the one observed in March-
EFY2016. |  |  |  | 
Beginning from March-EFY 2016 the magnitude of the inflation rate steadily declined up to March-EFY

EFY2016. |  |  |  |

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 3
--------------------------------------------------
Fig 1: Food and Non-Food Inflation (%, Year-on-Year), March EFY2016 - March EFY2017
Non-Food Inflation and Alcoholic Beverages (Year-on-Year)
As can be seen from the above figure, the non-food year-on-year inflation rate increased in March-
ETY2017 as compared to last month while the Food inflation decreased in magnitude. The Non-Food
inflation increased by 16.2 percent in the same period. Particularly, Alcoholic Beverages and Tobacco
increased by (15.2%); Housing, water, electricity & other fuel component (17.4%); Furnishings, house-
hold equipment & routine maintenance of the House (15.4%); Health (12.7%); Transport (32.5%); Com-
munication (2.0%); Education (23.3%); Restaurants and hotels (18.3%) and Miscellaneous goods and
services (18.4%) have contributed for the rise in non-food inflation rate in March EFY2017.

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 4
--------------------------------------------------
Major sub divisions in the food and non-food commodities (year-on-Year).
When we look at major division of food and non-food commodities, which holds large share in the
commodity basket, the magnitude of inflation rate at March EFY2017 showed a declining situation
for Food as compared to the previous month. From Food and Non-Alcoholic Beverage aspect, the
highest weight is hold by Bread and Cereals, Vegetables, Other-Food products, Non-Alcoholic Bev-
erage, and Meat; whose inflation at March EFY2017 is recorded an increase for Bread & Cereals
(1.2%); Vegetables (6.1%); Other-Food products (Food products not elsewhere classified) (26.7%);
Meat (17.8%) and Non-Alcoholic Beverages & coffee (50.2%).
Similarly, during March 2017 EFY the year-on-year inflation rate of the major Non- Food items of
Housing, Water, Electricity, Gas, & Other Fuels (17.4%); Restaurants and Hotels (18.3%); Alco-
holic Beverages & Tobacco (15.2%); Furnishings, Household Equipment & Routine Household
Maintenance of the House (15.4%) and Clothing and Footwear (8.7%).

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 5
--------------------------------------------------
Month-to-Month Inflation Rate
The month-to-month general inflation rate for March EFY2017 has increased by 2.7 percent compared to what
was observed in February EFY2017. During the same month, the food and non-alcoholic component of the infla-
tion increased by 2.5 percent whereas the non-food component for its part increased by 3.2 percent.
Table 3: General Inflation Rate (%, Month-to-Month), March EFY2016 - March EFY2017

Month

May 0.5

December 0.8
January 0.6
February 0.5
March-EFY 2017 2.7
Month-to-Month Inflation of Food and Non-Food categories: In March EFY2017, among the food components
of the CPI, Meat increased by (1.4%), Fish and sea food increased by (0.9%), Milk, Cheese and Eggs increased by
(5.7%), Oils and Fats increased by (7.5%), Food products n.e.c increased by (3.0%), Non-Alcoholic beverages and
coffee increased by (6.4%) on the month to month basis.

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 6
--------------------------------------------------
Looking at non-food items, the increase in month-to-month comparison was observed for Alcoholic
beverages and tobacco increased by (2.4%), Clothing and Footwear (2.8%), Furniture, Furnishing,
household equipment and routine maintenance of the house increased by (4.1%), Housing, water,
Electricity, gas and other fuels increased by (2.5%), Transport increased by (1.3%), Recreation and
culture increased by (3.1%), and Hotel and Restaurants (4.7%), Health increased by (6.4%), Commu-
nication increased by (2.9%), and Miscellaneous Goods and Services increased by (3.6%) showed an
increase during the month.
Fig 2: Food and Non-Food Inflation (%, Month-to-Month), March EFY2016 - March EFY2017

Page 7
--------------------------------------------------
3: 12-Month Moving Average General, Food and Non-food Inflation
The 12-month moving average figures provides a broader perspective on inflation trends over a longer period.
As it is indicated in Table below the country level overall inflation rate (annual change based on 12 months
Moving Average) was 17.9 percent in March EFY2017 as compared to the one observed in a similar period a
year ago. Since March-EFY2016 inflation has been relatively eased in the country.

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 9
--------------------------------------------------
4. Annual Inflation trend
Relatively low and reduction in inflation rate was observed between 2005 and 2009. Since
EFY2010 inflation rate stick to double digit.
Fig 3: Average Annual General inflation in Ethiopia since Ethiopian EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)
The average annual general inflation rate for the country stood at (26.6 %) in EFY2016 compared
to (32.6 %) in EFY2015. The past five years are unfavorable for the households, due to high infla-
tion in the country. Specially, in the year EFY2014 and EFY2016, the inflation rate has been over
30 percent for most months. Internal and external factors forced the inflation to be at higher lev-
el; COVID19, internal conflict, and Russia-Ukraine War were the major factors.

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 10
--------------------------------------------------
Fig 4: Average Annual Food Inflation in Ethiopia since EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)
The average annual food inflation rate for the country stood at (28.1 %) in EFY2016 compared to
(31.8 %) in EFY2015, about four percentage point reduction compared to last year figure. But
since 2010 the inflation rate stacked at double digit.

Issue No. 21/2017 Page 11
--------------------------------------------------
Fig 5: Average Annual Non-Food Inflation in Ethiopia since EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)
The average annual non-food inflation rate for the country stood at (24.3 %) in EFY2016 compared
to (33.8 %) in EFY2015. Non-food inflation movement, the past ten year divided into two. Between
2005 and 2009 non-food inflation showed reduction and since then the rate demonstrate increas-
ing trend. This is could be attached to Birr devaluation against USD, Russia-Ukraine War, and in-
crease of international price of gasoline, as most of non-food items are imported or utilize import-
ed raw materials. Relative easing of the non-food inflation for EFY2016 is observed as a result of
tight monetary policy in place to reduce bank credit.

| Issue No. 21/2017
Page 12
--------------------------------------------------
Enquiries | 
 | 5. GLOSSARY
• General Inflation: The highest level of aggregation of
inflation which covers all the items within the scope
of the inflation.
• Food Inflation: inflation which covers all food items
within the scope of the inflation.
• Non-food Inflation: inflation which covers all non-
food items within the scope of the inflation.
• Year-on-Year percentage change: The Yearly per-
centage change is the change in the index of the rele-
vant month of the current year compared with the
index of the same month in the previous year ex-
pressed as a percentage.
• Month-to-Month percentage change: The monthly
percentage change is the change in the index of the
relevant month compared with the index of the previ-
ous month expressed as a percentage.
• Index reference period (December EFY2009=100):
The period with which all other periods are compared
(the period for which the value of the CPI is set at
100).

         
                                                                                    
       Non-Food Inflation and Alcoholic Beverages (Year-on-Year)                    
                                                                                    
     As can be seen from the above figure, the non-food year-on-year inflation rate increased in March-
                                                                                    
     ETY2017 as compared to last month while the Food inflation decreased in magnitude. The Non-Food
     inflation increased by 16.2 percent in the same period. Particularly, Alcoholic Beverages and Tobacco
     increased by (15.2%); Housing, water, electricity & other fuel component (17.4%); Furnishings, house-
     hold equipment & routine maintenance of the House (15.4%); Health (12.7%); Transport (32.5%); Com-
     munication (2.0%); Education (23.3%); Restaurants and hotels (18.3%) and Miscellaneous goods and
     services (18.4%) have contributed for the rise in non-food inflation rate in March EFY2017.

       Major sub divisions in the food and non-food commodities (year-on-Year).     
                                                                                    
       When we look at major division of food and non-food commodities, which holds large share in the
       commodity basket, the magnitude of inflation rate at March EFY2017 showed a declining situation
                                                                                    
       for Food as compared to the previous month. From Food and Non-Alcoholic Beverage aspect, the
       highest weight is hold by Bread and Cereals, Vegetables, Other-Food products, Non-Alcoholic Bev-
                                                                                    
       erage, and Meat; whose inflation at March EFY2017 is recorded an increase for Bread & Cereals
       (1.2%); Vegetables (6.1%); Other-Food products (Food products not elsewhere classified) (26.7%);
                                                                                    
       Meat (17.8%) and Non-Alcoholic Beverages & coffee (50.2%).                   
       Similarly, during March 2017 EFY the year-on-year inflation rate of the major Non- Food items of
                                                                                    
       Housing, Water, Electricity, Gas, & Other Fuels (17.4%); Restaurants and Hotels (18.3%); Alco-
       holic Beverages & Tobacco (15.2%); Furnishings, Household Equipment & Routine Household
                                                                                    
       Maintenance of the House (15.4%) and Clothing and Footwear (8.7%).

      Month-to-Month   Inflation Rate                                               
                                                                                    
      The month-to-month general inflation rate for March EFY2017 has increased by 2.7 percent compared to what
                                                                                    
      was observed in February EFY2017. During the same month, the food and non-alcoholic component of the infla-
      tion increased by 2.5 percent whereas the non-food component for its part increased by 3.2 percent.
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          Table 3: General Inflation Rate (%, Month-to-Month), March EFY2016 - March EFY2017
                                                                                    
                                         General month-to-month inflation           
             Month                                                                  
             MarchEFY 2016                          4.0                             
             April                                 -0.7                             
             May                                    0.5                             
             June                                   0.9                             
             July                                   1.5                             
             August                                 1.2                             
             September                              2.4                             
             October                                0.9                             
             November                              -0.8                             
             December                               0.8                             
             January                                0.6                             
             February                               0.5                             
             March-EFY 2017                         2.7                             
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
      Month-to-Month Inflation of Food and Non-Food categories: In March EFY2017, among the food components
                                                                                    
      of the CPI, Meat increased by (1.4%), Fish and sea food increased by (0.9%), Milk, Cheese and Eggs increased by
      (5.7%), Oils and Fats increased by (7.5%), Food products n.e.c increased by (3.0%), Non-Alcoholic beverages and
                                                                                    
      coffee increased by (6.4%) on the month to month basis.

      beverages and tobacco increased by (2.4%), Clothing and Footwear (2.8%), Furniture, Furnishing,
                                                                                    
      household equipment and routine maintenance of the house increased by (4.1%), Housing, water,
      Electricity, gas and other fuels increased by (2.5%), Transport increased by (1.3%), Recreation and
                                                                                    
      culture increased by (3.1%), and Hotel and Restaurants (4.7%), Health increased by (6.4%), Commu-
      nication increased by (2.9%), and Miscellaneous Goods and Services increased by (3.6%) showed an
                                                                                    
      increase during the month.

         3: 12-Month Moving Average General, Food and Non-food Inflation            
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
        The 12-month moving average figures provides a broader perspective on inflation trends over a longer period.
                                                                                    
        As it is indicated in Table below the country level overall inflation rate (annual change based on 12 months
        Moving Average) was 17.9 percent in March EFY2017 as compared to the one observed in a similar period a
                                                                                    
        year ago. Since March-EFY2016 inflation has been relatively eased in the country.

        4. Annual Inflation trend                                                   
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
        Relatively low and reduction in inflation rate was observed between 2005 and 2009. Since
        EFY2010 inflation rate stick to double digit.                               
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
          Fig 3: Average Annual General inflation in Ethiopia since Ethiopian EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
         The average annual general inflation rate for the country stood at (26.6 %) in EFY2016 compared
                                                                                    
         to (32.6 %) in EFY2015. The past five years are unfavorable for the households, due to high infla-
         tion in the country. Specially, in the year EFY2014 and EFY2016, the inflation rate has been over
                                                                                    
         30 percent for most months. Internal and external factors forced the inflation to be at higher lev-
                                                                                    
         el; COVID19, internal conflict, and Russia-Ukraine War were the major factors.

                                                                                   
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
         The average annual food inflation rate for the country stood at (28.1 %) in EFY2016 compared to
                                                                                    
         (31.8 %) in EFY2015, about four percentage point reduction compared to last year figure. But
         since 2010 the inflation rate stacked at double digit.

                                                                      
                                                                                    
                                                                                    
        The average annual non-food inflation rate for the country stood at (24.3 %) in EFY2016 compared
        to (33.8 %) in EFY2015. Non-food inflation movement, the past ten year divided into two. Between
                                                                                    
        2005 and 2009 non-food inflation showed reduction and since then the rate demonstrate increas-
        ing trend. This is could be attached to Birr devaluation against USD, Russia-Ukraine War, and in-
                                                                                    
        crease of international price of gasoline, as most of non-food items are imported or utilize import-
                                                                                    
        ed raw materials. Relative easing of the non-food inflation for EFY2016 is observed as a result of
        tight monetary policy in place to reduce bank credit.

      You can visit us on the Website at:
